serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 22 June 1968 – gunshot wound to head and neck in contact at YS523586
derived_details.summary: Private Michael John Wickman of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 22 June 1968 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Wounded in action, 23 July 1969 – mine at Ap Lo Gom on Route 44. Died of wounds, 29 July 1969, from wounds recieved at Ap Lo Gom.
derived_details.summary: Private Thomas Noel Hollows of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 29 July 1968 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 14 September 1968 – gunshot wound in contact.
derived_details.summary: Private Brian John Petersen of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 14 September 1968 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Gunshot wound to throat in contact.
derived_details.summary: Lance Corporal David Ian Bensemann of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 16 November 1968 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 17 June 1969 – gunshot wounds to head and neck in contact, when V4 encountered a large enemy bunker system
derived_details.summary: Private John Stanley Williams of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 17 June 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 5 June 1969 – rocket propelled grenade fragments to head in contact.
derived_details.summary: Corporal John Richard Gatenby of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 5 June 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Nine weeks into his Tour of Duty, Don became very sick and died of illness on 11 July 1969
derived_details.summary: Private David Charles Frith of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 11 July 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Wounded in action, 23 July 1969 – mine at Ap Lo Gom on Route 44. Died of wounds, 4 August 1969 – as a result of wounds suffered at Ap Lo Gom.
derived_details.summary: Private Robert Alexander Hawthorne of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 4 August 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: New Zealand Engineers

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 12 August 1969 – mine.
derived_details.summary: Sapper Robert Henry Brown of the New Zealand Engineers was killed in action on 12 August 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Mine. Shrapnel wounds to groin and body. Died of wounds in Australia, 20 November 1969, as a result of wounds suffered 12 August 1969.
derived_details.summary: Private Anthony Vincent Bermingham of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 20 November 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: On 24 November 1969 he and three other members of V4 were on patrol with an ARVN platoon when they were ambushed. In the ensuing contact, Pete (who was the M60 machine gunner) was mortally wounded and Jerry Barrett killed. Two other New Zealanders – Ray Davidson and Patrick Smith – were wounded.
derived_details.summary: Private Patrick Rauhihi of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 24 November 1969 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: New Zealand Special Air Service

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action during a contact on patrol in Binh Tuy Province.
derived_details.summary: Sergeant Gordon John Campbell of the New Zealand SAS was killed in action on 14 January 1970 in Long Khanh Province during reconnaissance operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: Royal New Zealand Air Force

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 1 March 1970 – booby trap. Sgt Gordon Watt was the RNZAF's only fatal casualty of the Vietnam War. He remains the only air force member killed as a result of enemy action since 1945.
derived_details.summary: Sergeant Gary Stanley Watt of the RNZAF died on 1 March 1970 in South Vietnam due to an accidental incident.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: On 19 March 1970, less than two months before the end of his Tour of Duty, Mo was killed in a major contact during Operation Waiponamu in the Nui Dinh Hills.
derived_details.summary: Private Thomas Wiremu Paenga of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 19 March 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Wounded in action during operations in Phuoc Tuy province, 30 January 1970. Medevaced to Sydney, Australia but died of wounds, 19 March 1970.
derived_details.summary: Private David Noel Wright of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 19 March 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: On 17 April 1970 Stan's platoon were leading a company advance during Operation Townsville – V4's final operation – when they contacted the enemy and he was killed in action.
derived_details.summary: Lieutenant Stephen John Kidd of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 17 April 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 29 May 1970 – grenade booby trap.
derived_details.summary: Private John Lawrence Gurnick of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 29 May 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Died of wounds, 18 June 1970 – command detonated mine.
derived_details.summary: Lance Corporal Colin Robert Fisk of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 18 June 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 18 June 1970 – command detonated mine.
derived_details.summary: Private Leslie Charles Jones of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 18 June 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 31 October 1970 – gunshot wound to back in friendly fire incident while conducting a river crossing.
derived_details.summary: Lance Corporal Murray Lawrence Sutherland of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 31 October 1970 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Killed in action, 10 March 1971 – shrapnel from claymore mine detonated by fire in contact.
derived_details.summary: Lieutenant John Richard Winton of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 10 March 1971 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.

serviceRecordAuthority.unit: V Company, 1st Battalion, Royal New Zealand Infantry Regiment

derived_details.circumstances_of_death: Died of wounds at 2100 hrs in 24th US Evacuation Hospital, Long Binh – gunshot wound to head in contact.
derived_details.summary: Private Kenneth Henry Harding of V Company, 1RNZIR was killed in action on 24 June 1971 in Phuoc Tuy Province during operations.
